senses.definition: plural of prelimit, a term referring to something set before a limit is determined, often in legal or procedural contexts
examples: The document lists several prelimits to ensure safety before proceeding.
They discussed prelimits that must be met prior to approval.

senses.definition: informal short form of preliminaries; initial or introductory stage or events
examples: The conference starts with the prelims, then the main talks.
She prepared for the prelims before the final match.

senses.definition: existing or occurring before the acquisition of language (often in reference to deafness or language development)
examples: Prelingual deafness is present before speech sounds are learned.
The researcher studied prelingual factors in early language development.

senses.definition: in a prelingual manner; before language acquisition
examples: The condition affects individuals prelingually.
Prelingually deaf children may require early educational support.

senses.definition: relating to the period before language use or language development
examples: Prelinguistic sounds include babbling in infants.
Researchers study prelinguistic cues to language development.

senses.definition: a playful or nonsensical term; contextually may refer to a character name or invented concept
examples: In the story, prelinpinpin is a whimsical creature.
The company used the word as a code name: prelinpinpin.

senses.definition: to arrange to liquidate something before a deadline or before normal processing; to prepare for dissolution
examples: They plan to preliquidate the assets before closing the deal.
The team preliquidated inventory to clear space.

senses.definition: having been arranged for liquidation in advance
examples: The preliquidated assets were ready for sale.
Preliquidated funds were set aside.

senses.definition: the process of arranging liquidation in advance
examples: They are preliquidating inventory to reduce losses.
Preliquidating the portfolio helped balance risk.

senses.definition: the act or process of arranging liquidation beforehand
examples: Preliquidation planning is required for the bankruptcy filing.
They reviewed preliquidation steps for asset clearance.

senses.definition: plural of preliteracy; early literacy skills or foundations before formal reading/writing
examples: Early childhood programs focus on preliteracies like phonemic awareness.
Teachers assess preliteracies to tailor instruction.

senses.definition: the stage before formal reading and writing; early literacy readiness
examples: Preliteracy skills include recognizing sounds and letters.
Parents can support preliteracy at home.

senses.definition: of or relating to a time before literal language use; not yet literate
examples: Preliteral sounds occur before speech is codified into symbols.
The preliteral stage varies across cultures.

senses.definition: in a preliteral or non-literal way; before the stage of literal language
examples: The script uses language preliterally to evoke childhood imagery.
Preliterally they acted out the story.

senses.definition: the quality or state of existing before literal language
examples: The speaker relied on preliteralness to convey mood.
Preliteralness can affect early communication strategies.

senses.definition: relating to a period before literature; not yet literary
examples: Preliterary manuscripts were scarce.
The museum showcased preliterary texts.

senses.definition: existing before the development of writing systems; not literate
examples: Preliterate societies used memory and artifacts instead of writing.
Researchers study preliterate cultures to understand early communication.

senses.definition: plural of preliterate person or concept; individuals not yet literate
examples: The program targeted preliterate children.
Preliterate communities rely on oral tradition.

senses.definition: material that exists before formal literature; early writings or drafts
examples: Scholars review preliterature to trace literary development.
Preliterature includes proto-texts and drafts.

senses.definition: occurring before the use of lithic (stone) technology; earlier than stone age
examples: Prelithic tools were made from bone and wood.
Archaeologists study prelithic sites to understand early behavior.

senses.definition: the period before a lawsuit or formal legal action is filed
examples: They attempted a prelitigation negotiation to avoid court.
Prelitigation steps include gathering evidence.

senses.definition: the concept of prior lives or previous existence; often used in spiritual or philosophical contexts
examples: Some beliefs speak of prelives influencing current traits.
The novel explores prelives and karma.

senses.definition: to load something before it is needed; to put something in advance
examples: They preload the software with useful templates.
The app preloads images to speed up viewing.

senses.definition: an initial load or amount loaded in advance
examples: The preload of fuel was insufficient.
The game has a preload of assets to ensure smooth startup.

senses.definition: already loaded in advance
examples: The preloaded images appeared instantly.
Preloaded content made the app faster to use.

senses.definition: the act of loading something in advance
examples: Preloading images reduces wait time.
Preloading data helps performance.
